task: prepend_scheme_if_needed in utils.py prepends new_scheme to a URL that has no scheme, but must not replace a scheme that is already present. Simplify the scheme assignment in utils.py.
known_issue: Removing the `if scheme is None` guard makes the function assign new_scheme unconditionally, so it now overwrites a scheme that is already present — directly violating the documented contract ('Does not replace a present scheme'). A URL that already specifies https:// is rewritten to the caller's new_scheme (e.g. http://), silently downgrading the connection. requests uses this when normalising proxy URLs, so an https proxy target can be turned into http.
expected_correct_fix: Keep the guard so an existing scheme is preserved: if scheme is None: scheme = new_scheme.
